tasks.subject: [diff-viewer] [P1] Add the `diff` canvas variant, `open_diff` control_ui action, and diff DTOs to @dorkos/shared
tasks.description:
Extend `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts`:
- Add a `diff` variant to `UiCanvasContentSchema` (the discriminated union): `z.object({ type: z.literal('diff'), sourcePath: z.string(), mediaKind: z.enum(['text','image']).optional(), title: z.string().optional() })`. Add `diff` to the `UiStateSchema.canvas.contentType` enum. TSDoc the variant like its siblings (no bytes travel in the command; renderer loads baseline+current itself).
- Add an `open_diff` action to `UiCommandSchema`: `z.object({ action: z.literal('open_diff'), sourcePath: z.string() })`. This is part of the workbench 3-place recipe â€” the `sourcePath` input field already exists in `CONTROL_UI_INPUT`; ALSO extend `CONTROL_UI_DESCRIPTION` in `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/shared/ui-tool-contract.ts` with an `open_diff` line ("open a review diff for a file the you edited"). No separate Codex schema â€” parity is automatic via the shared contract.
- Add diff DTO schemas + inferred types: `DiffBaselineQuerySchema` ({ cwd, path, sessionId, mode?: 'session'|'head' }), `DiffBaselineResponseSchema` ({ baseline: string, baselineHash: string, current: string, currentHash: string, capturedFrom: 'pre-tool'|'reconstructed'|'head'|'empty' }), `AdvanceBaselineRequestSchema` ({ cwd, path, sessionId }), `DiffPendingQuerySchema`/`DiffPendingResponseSchema` ({ files: string[] }). Export via the appropriate `@dorkos/shared` subpath.
- In `packages/shared/src/viewer-registry.ts`, add a `diffMediaKindForPath(filePath, overrides?)` helper returning `'image'` when the resolved viewer is `image`, else `'text'` (diff only supports text+image; other types don't diff).

Tests (`packages/shared/src/__tests__/`): Zod round-trips for the `diff` variant, `open_diff` action, and every DTO; reject malformed; `diffMediaKindForPath` mapping. TSDoc on all exports. Do NOT hand-sort Tailwind/format â€” Prettier owns it.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the diff canvas variant, open_diff action, and DTOs to shared

tasks.subject: [diff-viewer] [P1] Build the per-session pre-edit baseline store (services/diff/edit-baseline.ts + git-baseline.ts)
tasks.description:
New server domain `apps/server/src/services/diff/`.

`edit-baseline.ts` â€” in-memory per-session baseline store. Shape: `Map<sessionId, Map<absPath, { bytes: Buffer, capturedAt: number, capturedFrom: 'pre-tool'|'reconstructed'|'head' }>>`. API:
- `captureFromDisk(sessionId, absPath)`: FIRST-TOUCH-WINS â€” if a baseline already exists for (sessionId, absPath), no-op and return it; else read current disk bytes as a Buffer and store with `capturedFrom: 'pre-tool'`. (Called BEFORE the edit applies â€” see task 1.3.)
- `captureReconstructed(sessionId, absPath, bytes)`: store a fallback baseline (`capturedFrom: 'reconstructed'`) only if none exists.
- `get(sessionId, absPath)`: return the baseline or undefined.
- `advance(sessionId, absPath, bytes)`: baseline := given bytes (finish-review; baseline=current disk).
- `listPending(sessionId)`: return absPaths whose stored baseline differs from current disk bytes.
- `clearSession(sessionId)`: drop the session's whole map.

`git-baseline.ts` â€” `gitShowHead(cwd, relPath): Promise<Buffer | null>` via `execFile('git', ['show', `HEAD:${relPath}`], { cwd, timeout })` (NO shell), reusing `validateBoundary` + the `execFile`+timeout idiom from `services/core/git-status.ts`; returns null for untracked/non-git.

`index.ts` barrel + module TSDoc.

Tests (`services/diff/__tests__/`): first-touch-wins (second capture is a no-op); `advance` replaces; `listPending` detects a changed file and ignores an unchanged one; `clearSession` drops everything; `gitShowHead` returns content for a tracked file and null for untracked/non-git (use a temp git repo fixture). Each test carries a purpose comment. `os.homedir()` is banned; take any needed data dir as a param.
tasks.activeForm: Building the per-session pre-edit baseline store

tasks.subject: [diff-viewer] [P1] Capture baselines at the claude-code pre-tool boundary and clear them on session teardown
tasks.description:
Wire baseline capture into the claude-code runtime so a file's pre-edit bytes are snapshotted BEFORE the SDK applies an edit.

- In `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/` (the PreToolUse hook / `canUseTool` seam â€” locate the exact synchronous pre-tool point that fires before the edit is written; the hook lifecycle already emits HookStartedEvent), for edit-family tools (`Edit`, `Write`, `MultiEdit`, `NotebookEdit`) resolve the tool input's `file_path` to an absolute path (cwd-confined via the existing boundary helper), and call `editBaseline.captureFromDisk(sessionId, absPath)`. First-touch-wins makes repeated edits cheap. This MUST run before the edit applies â€” verify ordering against the SDK hook semantics.
- Fallback ladder for when no pre-tool snapshot exists at read time is implemented in the route (task 1.4), NOT here â€” this task only wires the precise primary capture.
- Hook `editBaseline.clearSession(sessionId)` into the session's stream teardown / eviction path so the map never leaks across a closed session.
- The SDK import stays confined to `services/runtimes/claude-code/` (ESLint rule 2). Do not import the Claude SDK anywhere else.

Tests: with a `FakeAgentRuntime`-style harness or a focused unit around the hook adapter, assert an `Edit` tool call triggers `captureFromDisk` once with the resolved absolute path, that a second edit to the same file does not re-capture, and that session teardown calls `clearSession`. If the exact pre-tool seam proves async-after-apply for a given runtime, escalate per Open Question Q3 rather than silently capturing post-edit bytes.
tasks.activeForm: Wiring baseline capture at the claude-code pre-tool boundary

tasks.subject: [diff-viewer] [P1] Add routes/diff.ts: baseline resolution, advance, and pending (boundary-safe)
tasks.description:
New `apps/server/src/routes/diff.ts`, mounted under `/api/diff`. REUSE the boundary helpers from `routes/files.ts` (`resolveWithinCwd`, `assertAncestorWithin`, `sendPathError`) â€” extract them to a shared `lib/` module if cleaner, but do NOT reinvent path logic. Thin handlers: Zod-validate, call the service, map errors.

- `GET /api/diff/baseline` (`DiffBaselineQuerySchema`): resolve the baseline via the ladder â€” (1) `editBaseline.get(sessionId, absPath)` snapshot; (2) if absent and the first observed edit is reconstructable, reconstruct (Edit/MultiEdit reverse-apply against current disk â€” implement a `reconstructBaseline` helper; Write-first with no snapshot skips to 3); (3) `gitShowHead` if tracked; (4) empty (whole file = added). Read current disk text via the same size cap as `GET /api/files/content` (`FILE_LIMITS.MAX_TEXT_FILE_BYTES` â†’ 413; binary NUL â†’ 415). Return `{ baseline, baselineHash, current, currentHash, capturedFrom }`. `mode=head` forces step 3.
- `POST /api/diff/baseline/advance` (`AdvanceBaselineRequestSchema`): read current disk bytes, `editBaseline.advance(...)`, 200 `{ ok: true }`.
- `GET /api/diff/pending` (`DiffPendingQuerySchema`): `editBaseline.listPending(sessionId)` â†’ workspace-relative paths. (Nice-to-have â€” ship if it fits; the spec permits cutting it to a follow-up.)
- Register the router in the server's route table.

Tests (`routes/__tests__/diff.test.ts`): the full resolution ladder (snapshot / reconstructed[Edit] / HEAD / empty), each with a purpose comment; `Write`-first-no-snapshot falls to HEAD then empty; binary â†’ 415, oversize â†’ 413; **boundary-escape rejection on every route** â€” `..`, symlinked-parent (NON-mocked, real fs â€” the workbench chunk-A review caught a symlink-parent hole; replicate that real-escape test), null byte â†’ 400; `advance` writes-nothing-to-disk and only moves the in-memory baseline. Reverting is NOT in this route â€” the client reuses `PUT /api/files/content`.

tasks.subject: [diff-viewer] [P1] Add readDiffBaseline / advanceDiffBaseline transport methods on both HttpTransport and DirectTransport
tasks.description:
Extend the `Transport` interface (`packages/shared/src/transport.ts`) and both implementations:
- `readDiffBaseline(cwd, path, sessionId, mode?: 'session'|'head'): Promise<DiffBaselineResponse>` â€” HttpTransport calls `GET /api/diff/baseline`; DirectTransport resolves in-process (calls the same `services/diff` resolution used by the route, and `git-baseline` via child_process) so TEXT DIFF WORKS UNDER OBSIDIAN.
- `advanceDiffBaseline(cwd, path, sessionId): Promise<void>` â€” HttpTransport POSTs `/api/diff/baseline/advance`; DirectTransport calls the service directly.
- (`diffBaselineMediaUrl` is added in Chunk B, task 2.2 â€” not here.)
- Coded errors mirror the file methods (NOT_FOUND / TOO_LARGE / BINARY_FILE) so the client can message them.

TSDoc every new interface member. Tests: mock-fetch round-trip for HttpTransport; in-process path for DirectTransport (a temp cwd fixture). Reverting continues to use the shipped `writeFile` â€” do not add a new write method.
tasks.activeForm: Adding diff-baseline transport methods on both transports

tasks.subject: [diff-viewer] [P1] Add workbench.autoOpenDiff config field with a semver migration
tasks.description: Follow the `adding-config-fields` skill end-to-end. In `packages/shared/src/config-schema.ts`, add `autoOpenDiff: z.boolean().default(true)` to the `workbench` object (with TSDoc: when true, DorkOS auto-opens a diff document when the agent edits a file). Update the `workbench` block's `.default(() => (...))` to include `autoOpenDiff: true`. Add a semver-keyed migration in `apps/server/src/services/core/config-manager.ts` that backfills `workbench.autoOpenDiff` onto an existing `workbench` block (conf merges, mirror the `terminalGraceTtlMinutes` migration precedent). Update `packages/shared/src/__tests__/config-schema.test.ts` + the config-manager migration test, and `contributing/configuration.md`. No hand-formatting.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the workbench.autoOpenDiff config field + migration

tasks.subject: [diff-viewer] [P1] Build the text diff viewer (@codemirror/merge) with per-hunk accept/reject and the diff-review feature
tasks.description:
Add `@codemirror/merge` to `apps/client/package.json`. New FSD feature `apps/client/src/layers/features/diff-review/` (barrel `index.ts` with `@module` TSDoc):
- `ui/CanvasDiffContent.tsx` â€” props `{ content: Extract<UiCanvasContent, { type: 'diff' }>, documentId: string }`. Loads baseline+current via `useDiffReview` (below). Renders `@codemirror/merge` `unifiedMergeView` (DEFAULT â€” inline) with `original = baseline`, doc = current; language highlighting + theme reuse the existing `CodeMirrorEditor` setup (language Compartment + `@codemirror/language-data`, app CM theme). A header shows change/reviewed counts, Accept all / Reject all / Mark reviewed, a Side-by-side toggle (MergeView) shown only on wide viewports, and a "Compare against: Session start | Last commit" toggle (drives `mode`). Lazy-load the merge chunk via `React.lazy` (match `CodeMirrorEditor`/`BlintzCanvas`). Debounce live refetch on rapid agent edits.
- `model/use-diff-review.ts` â€” loads `readDiffBaseline` (TanStack Query, keyed by cwd+path+sessionId+mode); exposes `rejectHunk(hunk)`, `acceptHunk(hunk)`, `acceptAll()`, `rejectAll()`, `markReviewed()`. REJECT builds reverted full-file content (see lib/hunks) and writes via the shipped `useCanvasFileSave`/`writeFile` with `expectedHash` = currentHash; on 409 surface a calm "file changed â€” refresh" banner and recompute, NEVER blind-write. ACCEPT is client-only dismiss. `rejectAll` writes baseline; `markReviewed`/accept-all calls `advanceDiffBaseline`.
- `lib/hunks.ts` â€” pure: given baseline, current, and a chosen hunk (from the merge view's chunk model), produce the full-file content with that hunk reverted to baseline. Unit-tested independently.
- Wire the `@codemirror/merge` per-chunk accept/reject gutter callbacks to `acceptHunk`/`rejectHunk` (intercept â€” do NOT let it mutate the editor doc directly).

Canvas integration: in `layers/features/canvas/ui/`, dispatch the `diff` content type to `CanvasDiffContent` (featureâ†’feature UI composition is allowed; the diff feature's MODEL must not import canvas internals). Add the `diff` case to `app-store-canvas.ts` `sourceKey` (`diff:<sourcePath>`), `sourceLabel` (baseName), and `CONTENT_TYPE_FALLBACK_LABELS`. Add the `open_diff` case to `ui-action-dispatcher.ts` (`executeUiCommand`): resolve mediaKind via `diffMediaKindForPath`, `openCanvasDocument({ type: 'diff', sourcePath, mediaKind })`, then `revealCanvas(store, origin)` â€” respecting origin (agent = view-only tab setter).

Tests (RTL + mock Transport): reject-hunk calls writeFile with reverted content + expectedHash and on a mocked 409 shows the banner and does not clobber; accept-hunk writes nothing; accept-all/reject-all; markReviewed calls advanceDiffBaseline; `lib/hunks` reverts the right region; the `diff` variant coalesces (a second open of the same path does not add a tab). FSD: barrel imports only; TSDoc on exports.
tasks.activeForm: Building the text diff viewer with per-hunk accept/reject

tasks.subject: [diff-viewer] [P1] Auto-open a diff document when the attached session's agent edits a file
tasks.description:
New `apps/client/src/layers/features/diff-review/model/use-auto-open-diff.ts`: subscribe via `streamManager.subscribeSessionEvent` to completed `tool_call` events (or the paired tool_result) whose `toolName` is edit-family (`Edit`/`Write`/`MultiEdit`/`NotebookEdit`) AND whose `sessionId === streamManager.attachedSessionId` (gate EXACTLY like the `ui_command` dispatch so a background agent can't pop a diff over the foreground session). Parse `file_path` from the tool `input` JSON, and dispatch `open_diff` at origin `'agent'` (view-only tab setter â€” never overwrite the user's per-agent right-panel tab preference, DOR-227). Coalescing is free: the store dedups `diff:<sourcePath>`.

Gate the whole subscriber on the `workbench.autoOpenDiff` config flag (default true). Wire it once at the app shell (`apps/client/src/main.tsx`) beside the existing `streamManager.subscribeUiCommand(...)` wiring â€” an app-lifetime subscription, never torn down.

Tests (RTL + mock stream): an `Edit` completion for the attached session opens a `diff` doc; an edit for a DIFFERENT session is ignored; a non-edit tool (`Bash`/`Read`) is ignored; a second edit to the same file coalesces (no second tab); with `autoOpenDiff=false` nothing opens; the agent-origin open leaves the user's pinned right-panel tab unchanged.
tasks.activeForm: Auto-opening a diff document on agent file edits

tasks.subject: [diff-viewer] [P2] Serve baseline image bytes: GET /api/diff/baseline/raw
tasks.description:
Add `GET /api/diff/baseline/raw` to `apps/server/src/routes/diff.ts`. Serve the captured BASELINE image bytes for (sessionId, path) from the `editBaseline` snapshot (Buffer). Inherit the `GET /api/files/raw` posture EXACTLY: the narrow `MEDIA_CONTENT_TYPES` allowlist (415 for anything else â€” never an arbitrary-file reader), `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `Content-Disposition: inline`, `Cache-Control: private, max-age=0, must-revalidate`, and the SVG script-neutering CSP sandbox. Boundary-validate cwd+path via the same helpers. If no baseline snapshot exists (e.g. a git-tracked pre-existing image), fall back to `gitShowHead` bytes; else 404. Current image bytes continue to be served by the shipped `GET /api/files/raw` (no change).

Tests: serves stored baseline bytes with the right content-type; 415 for a non-media extension; 404 when no baseline and untracked; boundary-escape rejection (real fs, not mocked); SVG CSP header present.
tasks.activeForm: Serving baseline image bytes for the image diff

tasks.subject: [diff-viewer] [P2] Add diffBaselineMediaUrl transport method (web-only, null under DirectTransport)
tasks.description: Add `diffBaselineMediaUrl(cwd, path, sessionId): string | null` to the `Transport` interface and both impls. HttpTransport builds the `GET /api/diff/baseline/raw` URL (mirror `mediaUrl` in `system-methods.ts`). DirectTransport returns `null` (no in-process HTTP surface for a webview to fetch â€” mirrors the shipped `mediaUrl` gap, so IMAGE DIFF IS WEB-ONLY, consistent with the shipped image viewer). TSDoc the method noting the DirectTransport null contract. Test: HttpTransport returns a URL carrying cwd+path+sessionId; DirectTransport returns null.

tasks.subject: [diff-viewer] [P2] Build CanvasImageDiffContent with 2-up / swipe / onion-skin modes
tasks.description: New `apps/client/src/layers/features/diff-review/ui/CanvasImageDiffContent.tsx`, dispatched from the canvas when a `diff` document resolves to `mediaKind: 'image'`. Two `` layers: baseline via `transport.diffBaselineMediaUrl(...)`, current via the shipped `transport.mediaUrl(...)`. Three modes (switcher in the header): - 2-up â€” side by side; stacks vertically on narrow panels. - Swipe â€” a draggable vertical divider clips the top (current) layer via CSS `clip-path`/inset; pointer-drag to reveal baseline underneath. - Onion-skin â€” an opacity slider cross-fades baselineâ†”current. Use plain CSS + `motion` for the divider/slider â€” no new heavy dependency. When `diffBaselineMediaUrl` is null (DirectTransport) render a calm "Image diff isn't available here" message (web-only). Accept/reject for an image is whole-file: Accept keeps the current image (dismiss + advanceDiffBaseline); Reject writes the baseline bytes back to disk (fetch baseline bytes â†’ `writeFile` as binary, respecting the optimistic-concurrency contract) â€” reuse the diff-review model where practical. Tests (RTL + mock Transport): all three modes render both layers; the mode switch works; reject fetches baseline and writes it; under a DirectTransport mock (null baseline URL) the web-only message shows and no image layers mount. FSD/barrel/TSDoc rules apply.
tasks.activeForm: Building the image diff viewer with 2-up/swipe/onion-skin modes
